title: Arcypteris irregularis
taxonomy: Arcypteris irregularis is a fossil plant, and its classification falls within the division of plants known as the Pteridosperms or seed ferns. This group of plants is characterized by the presence of seed-like structures and is thought to have been one of the earliest groups of vascular plants to produce seeds. The Pteridosperms are further divided into several orders, with the order Corystospermales being the closest match for the classification of Arcypteris irregularis. This species is often associated with other fossil plants of the Corystospermales order, which are known for their distinctive leaf morphology. Arcypteris irregularis is thought to have lived during the Early Carboniferous period, approximately 330-320 million years ago.
